title: Senalak
content:
{{Top|ga|leg}} {{Plant |image= |name=Senalak |designation= |class= |origin= |found=*[[Selvaris]]''[[The New Jedi Order: The Unifying Force]]'' *''[[Baanu Rass]]''''[[The New Jedi Order: Star by Star]]'' |habitat= |lifespan= |description=*[[Limb/Legends|Knee]]-high *Barbed roots *Ability to unleash a strand of thorns when disturbed |edible= |inedible= |nutrition= |effects= |affiliation=[[Yuuzhan Vong]] }} {{Quote|The Shamed are naught but nuisance flies,
At least as seen through Shimrra's eyes;
The ''Jeedai'' are the ones he mourns,
Edged and sharp as senalak thorns.|[[Shamed Ones|Shamed One]] [[Onimi]]|}} '''Senalaks''' were [[Plant/Legends|plants]] sometimes deployed as security measures by the [[Yuuzhan Vong]]. Consisting of a thin, [[Limb/Legends|knee]]-high stalk, senalaks, when disturbed, released a [[Meter/Legends|meter]]-long thorny cord that would flail around, capturing or [[Death/Legends|killing]] nearby beings. During the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]], senalak fields were used on the [[Koros-Strohna|worldship]] ''[[Baanu Rass]]'' and later around a [[Selvaris camp|prisoner-of-war camp]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Selvaris]] as security fields. ==Biology and appearance== Senalaks were [[Plant/Legends|plants]] composed of [[Limb/Legends|knee]]-high stalks as thin as a finger that were topped with a [[Color/Legends|blue]] cap. When disturbed by a foreign object, the cap released a [[Meter/Legends|meter]]-long strand of barbed cord that would whip around for a few [[Standard second|seconds]].{{CSWECite|III|137|senalak}} Senalak roots were nearly as tangled and thorny as the barbed cords that the cap released and reached a fair distance under the surface of the ground. Growing in fields and able to be cultivated, senalaks could survive both in a [[Vacuum/Legends|vacuum]] or on a terrestrial surface, and they thrived in the tropical jungles of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Selvaris]]. Due to their coloration and height, senalaks resembled [[Water/Legends|ice]] spikes. ==History== During the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]]—fought primarily between the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] and the [[Yuuzhan Vong galaxy|extra-galactic]] [[Yuuzhan Vong]] [[Species/Legends|species]] for control of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]—the invading Yuuzhan Vong used senalaks as security fields around important land structures, relying on the barbed cords of the plants to [[Death/Legends|kill]] or capture any intruders. In [[27 ABY/Legends|27 ABY]],''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' dates the events of ''[[The New Jedi Order: Star by Star]]'' to [[27 ABY/Legends|27 ABY]]. senalaks were deployed around the [[Spaceport/Legends|spaceport]] of the Yuuzhan Vong [[Koros-Strohna|worldship]] ''[[Baanu Rass]]'', though the field was compromised by a [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Myrkr strike team|team]] that used [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] to push the stalks down, prompting the senalaks to release their strands of thorns before anyone reached them. At the end of the war on Selvaris, despite the senalaks cultivated around a Yuuzhan Vong [[Selvaris camp|prisoner-of-war camp]] located there, a quartet of [[Prisoner/Legends|prisoners]] managed to escape the camp after digging a tunnel underneath the field. ==Behind the scenes== Senalaks first appeared in the [[2001]] novel [[The New Jedi Order: Star by Star|''Star by Star'']], an installment of [[Star Wars: The New Jedi Order|''The New Jedi Order'']] series by author [[Troy Denning]]. The plants also received an entry in [[2008]]'s ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''. The encyclopedia incorrectly states that the Jedi team encountered senalaks on the planet [[Myrkr/Legends|Myrkr]] rather than aboard the ''Baanu Rass'', which was in Myrkyr's [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]] at the [[Time/Legends|time]], as presented in ''Star by Star''. ==Appearances== *''[[The New Jedi Order: Star by Star]]'' {{1st}} *''[[The New Jedi Order: The Unifying Force]]'' ==Sources== *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} [[Category:Yuuzhan Vong plants]] [[Category:Yuuzhan Vong weapons]]
